shortDefinition: A capitalization style where the first letter of a sentence is uppercase and the rest are mostly lowercase.
definition: Sentence case is a writing format in which each sentence starts with an uppercase letter while most other letters remain lowercase, improving readability for paragraphs and long-form content.

faqs.q: When should I use sentence case?
faqs.a: Use sentence case for most paragraphs, blog copy, and conversational text where readability is the top priority.

faqs.q: Is sentence case better than title case?
faqs.a: For body content, sentence case is usually clearer. For headings, title case may be preferred depending on style guidelines.

shortDefinition: A capitalization style where major words are capitalized, often used in headings and titles.
definition: Title case capitalizes principal words in a heading, making titles visually distinct and easier to scan in listings, blog cards, and navigation labels.

faqs.q: Where is title case most useful?
faqs.a: Title case is most useful for headlines, UI labels, and section titles where quick visual scanning matters.

examples.label: Input
examples.value: how to format text for seo

examples.label: Output
examples.value: How to Format Text for SEO

shortDefinition: The URL-friendly part of a web address that identifies a page using readable keywords.
definition: A slug is a clean URL segment made from meaningful words, typically lowercase and hyphen-separated, helping both users and search systems understand page context.

faqs.q: Why do slugs matter for discoverability?
faqs.a: Descriptive slugs improve URL clarity, retrieval relevance, and can increase citation quality in AI-generated answers.

shortDefinition: A format where words are joined without spaces and each word after the first begins with an uppercase letter.
definition: Camel case is commonly used for variable and function names in programming. The first word starts lowercase and subsequent words begin with uppercase letters.

faqs.q: What is the difference between camel case and pascal case?
faqs.a: Camel case starts with a lowercase word, while pascal case starts with an uppercase word.

shortDefinition: A format where words are joined and every word begins with an uppercase letter.
definition: Pascal case is a naming convention often used for class names and components in codebases, with each concatenated word capitalized.

faqs.q: Where is pascal case commonly used?
faqs.a: Pascal case is widely used in component and class naming conventions in many programming frameworks.

shortDefinition: A lowercase, hyphen-separated format used for URLs, file names, and semantic identifiers.
definition: Kebab case writes words in lowercase separated by hyphens. It is highly readable and commonly used in URLs, slugs, and CSS class naming.

faqs.q: Is kebab case good for URLs?
faqs.a: Yes. Kebab case is one of the most readable and search-friendly URL formatting styles.

shortDefinition: A lowercase format where words are separated with underscores.
definition: Snake case joins words with underscores and is common in database fields, configuration keys, and some programming ecosystems.

faqs.q: When should I choose snake case?
faqs.a: Choose snake case when your platform, language, or team conventions prefer underscore-separated naming.

shortDefinition: The process of transforming raw text into clearer, standardized, and purpose-fit output.
definition: Text formatting includes capitalization, spacing cleanup, punctuation normalization, and structure changes to improve readability, consistency, and publication quality.

faqs.a: Clean formatting improves readability for people and improves extraction quality for search engines and AI systems.

examples.label: Use cases
examples.value: SEO drafts, emails, social posts, blog editing
